narrative: The most striking feature in this morning's data is the divergence between energy and broad emerging markets: XLE has shed 5.9% over five days while EEM has gained 5.4%, suggesting capital is rotating away from commodity-linked exposure and toward EM growth names, with Japan (EWJ +5.0%) and tech (XLK +4.5%) adding to that risk-on tilt in a broadly calm environment. The stress score's rise from 13 to 20 is modest but worth noting as the one sign of incremental friction beneath an otherwise orderly surface. Watch whether crude prices stabilise or extend their decline, as a continued move lower would test whether the EM rally can hold without the commodity tailwind that has historically supported it.
